title: Issues, Reviews, Pull Requests, and Team Workflow · Conflict Triage
summary: Move a change from issue to review to integration with traceable decisions, bounded branch scope, and respectful collaboration. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a team contribution and review protocol and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.
Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.
Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Integration Policy evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Integration Policy assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Integration Policy has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Integration Policy precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Issue 3 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

Inspect the Workflow Policy trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.

content.text: For Workflow Policy, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Workflow Policy precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Issue 3 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One branching model is best for every team and release cadence.

stimulus.title: Workflow Policy case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Issue 3 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One branching model is best for every team and release cadence.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints. Artifact under review: a team contribution and review protocol.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Workflow Policy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.instruction:
debug case — Issue 4 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Workflow Policy symptom.

content.value: githooks Manual — Controls client and server hook invocation, environment, parameters, bypass, and exit behavior.

lines.label: required

options.text: Another successful run of the same happy-path example.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-integration-policy-rljqe8" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Integration Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-integration-policy-rljqe8
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Integration Policy precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Issue 4 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Passing CI alone grants authority to merge a change.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Integration Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Issue 5 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
